---
title: "Isolate_Categories"
author: "Hannah Whitehurst"
date: "11/7/2020"
output: html_document
runtime: shiny
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(shiny)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:


```{r eruptions, echo=FALSE}
iso_df <- read.csv("/Users/hannahwhitehurst/Documents/Isolate_Docs/FH-HK-HW.csv")

iso_df_gen <- as.character(unique(iso_df$Genus))

unique()
selectInput(
  'Genus', label = 'genus',
  choices = c(iso_df_gen), 
  selected = 'Sphingomonas',
  multiple = TRUE
)

renderPlot({
  par(mar = c(4, 4, .1, .5))
  hist(
    faithful$eruptions, as.numeric(input$breaks),
    col = 'gray', border = 'white',
    xlab = 'Duration (minutes)', main = ''
  )
})

server = function(input, output) {
    output$data <- renderTable({
      iso_df[, c(input$Genus), drop = FALSE]
    }, rownames = TRUE)
  }

#######
iso_df_gen <- as.character(unique(iso_df$Genus))

shinyApp(
  ui = fluidPage(

    selectInput(
   'Genus', label = 'genus',
    choices = c(iso_df_gen), 
    selected = 'Sphingomonas',
    multiple = TRUE),
    plotOutput("Plot"),
    tableOutput("data")

  ),
  server = function(input, output) {
  
  filtered_data <- reactive({
   df <- iso_df[iso_df[, "Genus"] == input$Genus,] #ok
   df <- df[,c("Genus","population.site")] %>% #ok 
         droplevels() %>% #ok
          table() %>% #ok
         data.frame() #ok
   df <- df[with(df, order(Genus)),] #ok
  
  }
  )
  
  output$Plot <- renderPlot({
   g<-ggplot(data=filtered_data(), aes(y=Genus, x=Freq)) +
        geom_col(aes(fill=population.site))
   g
   }
   )
    
  output$data <- renderTable({
  iso_df[iso_df[, "Genus"] == input$Genus,]
  }, rownames = TRUE)
  
  

  


}
)

   
  

  output$chart <- renderPlot({
    sum_counts <- iso_df[iso_df[, "Genus"] == input$Genus, c("Genus","population.site")] %>% 
      table()
        ggplot(sum_counts, aes(y=Genus, x=Freq)) +
        geom_col(aes(fill=population.site))
      },
    res =96
    )


testing <- iso_df[,c("Genus","population.site")] %>% 
  table()
ggplot(testing, aes(y=Genus, x=Freq)) +
geom_col(aes(fill=population.site))


sum.chart <-ggplot(iso_df[iso_df[, "Genus"] == c("Sphingomonas"), c("Genus","population.site")] %>% table, aes(y=Genus, x=Freq)) +
      geom_col(aes(fill=population.site))



####works
iso_df_gen <- as.character(unique(iso_df$Genus))

shinyApp(
  ui = fluidPage(

    selectInput(
   'Genus', label = 'genus',
    choices = c(iso_df_gen), 
    selected = 'Sphingomonas',
    multiple = TRUE),
    tableOutput("data")
  ),
  server = function(input, output) {
  #output$data <- renderTable({
  #iso_df[iso_df[, "Genus"] == input$Genus,]
  #  }, rownames = TRUE)
  
  output$data <- reactive({
   df <- iso_df[iso_df[, "Genus"] == input$Genus,] #ok
   df <- df[,c("Genus","population.site")] %>% #ok 
         droplevels() %>% #ok
          table() %>% #ok
         data.frame() #ok
   df <- df[with(df, order(Genus)),] #ok
  
  },
  rownames=TRUE
  )
  
  output$data <- renderPlot({
   
   #g<-ggplot(data=filtered_data(), aes(y=Genus, x=Freq)) +
    #    geom_col(aes(fill=population.site))
   #g
  }
  )



```


## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
